agreement.short_name: EAEU Unified Scheme of Tariff Preferences (Russia module)
agreement.full_name: EAEU Unified System of Tariff Preferences (USTP) - covers Russia, Armenia, Belarus, Kazakhstan, Kyrgyzstan as per Module Register (one shared module, 5 arrangements)

notes_before_sheets: Per document B/Schema Companion Guide: Russia GSP uses RULE-BASED eligibility (coverage_rules table), not a per-line positive/negative flag alone. Column G 'Pakistan USTP Eligibility' is the manually-applied flag described in the Companion Guide as something that 'can drift from the legal rules' -> load into tariff_lines.stated_eligibility, and separately populate coverage_rules from the module's 'Eligible Goods List' sheet (NOT yet inspected in this pass - FLAGGED for follow-up: need to confirm exact sheet name/columns for the Eligible Goods List in this workbook before coverage_rules can be loaded). Confirmed identical column layout across GSP_ARM_S.xlsx, GSP_BLR_S.xlsx, GSP_KAZ_S.xlsx, GSP_KGZ_S.xlsx, GSP_RUS_S.xlsx by direct inspection - one config serves all 5 per Module Register's stated grouping.

tariff_sheets.import_country: EAEU (Russia/Armenia/Belarus/Kazakhstan/Kyrgyzstan - see member_country per source file)
tariff_sheets.member_country: SET PER SOURCE FILE: Russia | Armenia | Belarus | Kazakhstan | Kyrgyzstan

columns._extra_note: Column B 'HS Formatted' and Column G 'Pakistan USTP Eligibility' and Column E 'UOM' have no direct schema field mapping decided yet - FLAGGED for review: should G map to stated_eligibility and E to a notes/remarks field, or be dropped as redundant? Column D 'Product Description (Russian source)' - FLAGGED: keep as product_desc alternate/secondary, or discard as non-English duplicate? Needs a decision from someone with domain knowledge, not assumed here.
